exemplar.title: Predicting the Winner and Runner-up for the Mobile Legends: Bang Bang Professional League Malaysia (MPL MY) Season 14

detailedExemplarData.title: Predicting the Winner and Runner-up for the Mobile Legends: Bang Bang Professional League Malaysia (MPL MY) Season 14

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear overall narrative structure with introduction, methods, evaluation and conclusion
Appropriate use of headings, numbered tables and reference list to guide the reader
Logical progression in most sections linking the research question to mathematical analysis
weaknesses: Mid-section digressions (repeated game-terminology, raw console output) interrupt flow
Formatting glitches (duplicated tables, broken LaTeX, misaligned matrices) detract from polish
Some algebraic passages (matrix inverse discussion) become confused and hamper clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands in band 3 (coherence and structure) and one in band 2 (conciseness) warrant an overall mark of 3.  The exploration is coherent and well framed but hampered by unnecessary material and formatting errors that prevent it reaching the highest level.

strengths: Correct use of key symbols (E[Y], Î», RMSD) and matrix notation in places
Effective tabular representation of prediction errors (Table 13)
Explanations are generally understandable and guide the reader through the computations
weaknesses: Notation is inconsistently applied (mixed bold, TeX fragments, typesetting errors)
Lack of graphical or visual plots limits multiple representations
Typographical mistakes in formulae and code blocks reduce precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall solidly in band 2.  The student uses some appropriate language and representations, and the communication is mostly clear, but inconsistent notation and absence of visualisations prevent a higher mark.

strengths: Genuine personal interest in Mobile Legends drives the choice of topic and data collection
Clear personal commentary appears throughout, linking steps back to studentâs experience
Development of two distinct models shows initiative beyond routine exercises
weaknesses: Mathematical depth is moderate rather than outstanding
Personal approach, while clear, does not fundamentally reshape the mathematical direction
Creativity is significant but not sustained at an exceptional level throughout
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each of the three engagement strands sits in band 2.  The work shows significant independent thinking and a personal approach, but it does not achieve the coherence of an outstanding, highly personal exploration.

strengths: Meaningful reflection on model accuracy using RMSD and identification of limitations (relegations, missing data)
Comparison of two models includes basic critique and quantitative analysis
Connections back to the research question are explicit, noting which model performs better
weaknesses: Reflection tends to be descriptive rather than deeply critical or forward-looking
Critical analysis of NNLS assumptions and weight choices is superficial
Next-step suggestions and sensitivity analyses are hinted at but not developed
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All reflection strands are consistently at band 2.  The student offers meaningful reflections and some critique, but these remain basic and not sufficiently deep to reach band 3.

strengths: Application of matrix least-squares, NNLS and Poisson modelling demonstrates sophisticated techniques at or beyond the HL syllabus
Two distinct models are constructed, compared quantitatively via RMSD and iteratively refined
Rigorous treatment of real-world data with systematic data collection underpins the mathematical work
weaknesses: Some algebraic mis-statements in the 3Ã3 matrix example weaken theoretical justification
Minor computational errors in matrix manipulation and code syntax issues appear
Glosses over certain distributional assumptions and regularization alternatives
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite a few technical slips, the exploration employs a high level of mathematical sophisticationâmultiple advanced modelling strategies, error-measure comparisons and statistical reasoningâfulfilling the descriptor for sophisticated, rigorous mathematics.  Awarding the maximum of 6 reflects the centrality and strength of mathematical content in this work.

strandSelections.reasoning: **Independent thinking:**  Choosing an e-sports league, compiling raw Season 13 data and devising two statistical models show clear student initiative beyond textbook examples.  The work is not routine, but the mathematical depth is moderate, so the evidence aligns with âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ independent thinking.

**Knowledge & understanding:**  The student explains key ideas (expected margin, Î» in Poisson, RMSD meaning) showing good understanding in parts, yet glosses over essential conditions (independence, suitability of NNLS) and occasionally misinterprets results (e.g. negative ratings).  This indicates solid but not thorough understanding, warranting 3 marks.

**Accuracy & correctness:**  Many computations (averages, RMSD) are correct, and RStudio outputs sensible vectors.  Nonetheless, the earlier 3 Ã 3 matrix example contains algebraic mis-statements and the NNLS justification is mathematically shaky.  With âmostly correctâ work but several substantive errors, the band for 3 marks fits best.

**Conciseness & relevance:**  Most material is related to predicting MPL standings, yet the write-up is verbose, with repeated tables (Table 7/13), long game-terminology sections and raw console output that add little mathematical value.  These unnecessary elements mean the exploration is not âmostly conciseâ, fitting better with âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements.â

**Mathematical language & notation:**  Correct symbols ( E[Y], Î», RMSD ) and matrix notation are attempted, but notation is inconsistent (mixed use of bold, TeX fragments, subscripts such as â$X<sup>T</sup>$â).  A number of typesetting errors (e.g. â$r< - solve (A, b)$â) further reduce clarity.  Overall there is âsome appropriateâ but not âmostly appropriateâ language.

**Clarity & consistency:**  Explanations are understandable, yet frequent typographical issues and inconsistent symbol usage create confusion for the reader.  Hence communication is âmostly clear with some consistencyâ rather than fully clear and consistent.

**Relevance & level of mathematics:**  Matrix least-squares, NNLS, Poisson probabilities and RMSD are all within or slightly beyond AI HL syllabus and are applied directly to the problem.  Two distinct models are constructed and used, showing effective application but not reaching the sophistication/rigour of advanced statistical modelling; hence 4 marks are appropriate.

**Multiple representations:**  The student supplies many tables, a few matrices and formulae; however, there are no graphs or visual summaries of predicted versus actual rankings.  Representations are therefore present but limited in variety and sometimes poorly formatted, matching the âsome appropriate representationsâ descriptor.

**Depth of reflection:**  The student reflects on model accuracy using RMSD and discusses limitations such as relegations and missing data.  Reflection appears at several points but is descriptive rather than deeply critical, matching âmeaningful reflection throughoutâ.

**Creativity & initiative:**  Applying least-squares ranking plus a weighted Poisson model to an e-sports dataset demonstrates noticeable creativity.  Still, the methods themselves are standard and the implementation contains errors, so the strand sits comfortably in the âsignificant creativityâ band, not âoutstandingâ.

**Coherence & logical development:**  The IA follows a recognisable narrative (introduction â data â two models â evaluation â conclusion).  Arguments generally build on previous sections and the reader can see why each piece of mathematics is introduced.  However, several mid-section digressions (e.g. repeated explanations of MLBB terms, long screenshots of raw Excel output) interrupt the flow, and some algebraic passages become confused (the matrix re-write and âno inverseâ discussion).  These weaknesses prevent the exploration from reaching the âhighly coherentâ descriptor but it is still clearly more than âsome coherenceâ.

**Critical analysis:**  Comparison of two models and quantitative error analysis provide basic critique; however, the treatment of matrix issues and NNLS assumptions is superficial.  Thus the analysis is âmeaningful with some critiqueâ rather than deep.

**Connection to understanding:**  The student links reflections back to the research question, explicitly stating which model better predicts winners and why.  These connections are clear but not strongly developed, fitting the second band.

**Organisation & structure:**  Headings, sub-headings, numbered tables and a closing reference list give the work a solid frame with clear introduction, body and conclusion.  Each method is separated and evaluated, and the conclusion revisits the research question.  Nevertheless, formatting glitches (duplicated tables, broken LaTeX, mis-aligned matrices) disturb the otherwise neat structure so the work cannot be described as âexceptionally well organisedâ.

**Personal approach:**  The exploration is driven by the studentâs long-held interest in Mobile Legends, and personal commentary appears throughout.  This genuine engagement guides choices of variables and evaluation, satisfying âclear personal approach,â but its impact on mathematical depth is limited.

annotations.comment: The personal anecdote in the introduction demonstrates genuine engagement, but it extends beyond essential context and could be condensed to maintain focus on the mathematical analysis.
The research question is clearly stated, providing a focused objective that guides the exploration. This clarity helps the reader understand the purpose from the outset.
The studentâs passion for MLBB is evident and drives the research question, showing clear personal engagement with the topic.
Guidelines for converting expected values to match scores show logical structure, but the inequality in the second bullet is mistyped (\(0 â¤ E[Y] â¤ -1\)); this should be corrected.
Predicted league tables are clearly presented, but the repetitive use of nearly identical tables could be streamlined or compared side by side for efficiency.
The Poisson formula $$P(X=x)=\frac{\lambda^x e^{-\lambda}}{x!}$$ is correctly stated. Explicitly stating the assumption of independent events strengthens mathematical rigor.
Compute and present population mean and standard deviation correctly, but note the dash in the mu+sigma row for Deaths; that blank entry should be addressed or justified.
Calculating weighted performance scores demonstrates creativity and initiative in synthesizing probabilities into a single metric, showing significant personal engagement.
The choice of weights (e.g. kills 30%, towers 25%) is not justified; including reasoning or sensitivity analysis would deepen critical reflection.
Table 13 effectively summarizes prediction errors by team, facilitating clear comparison of methods; this is a strong use of representation.
The league position comparison table contains empty rows and inconsistent row grouping; tidying the layout would improve coherence and presentation.
The RMSD formula is correctly applied and demonstrates solid understanding of error measurement; connecting this back to model assumptions shows rigour.
Table 1 definitions are detailed but overly verbose; consider summarizing terms more concisely or moving extended definitions to an appendix to improve conciseness.
The methodology section clearly outlines data sources, demonstrating the studentâs independent thinking and initiative in gathering real-world data.
Raw tables are used effectively, but the absence of graphical visualizations limits multiple representations; adding plots of distributions or predicted vs actual would enhance clarity.
Data collection is comprehensive and systematic, strengthening the foundation for subsequent analysis.
The equation $$E[Y] = r_a - r_b$$ is correctly introduced, but the expectation operator and notation could be clarified by defining $$Y$$ explicitly and specifying distributional assumptions.

The hypothetical matrix example abruptly changes a row to ones to force invertibility, which undermines the least squares modelâs theoretical justification; a better approach is to impose a constraint or use regularization.
Notation in the matrix computations is inconsistent (e.g. mixed bold, superscripts). Standardize notation and typesetting using LaTeX for clarity and consistency.
Recognizing the outlier rating for Bountee Esports and applying NNLS shows meaningful reflection on model limitations and corrective initiative.
The R code block contains syntax errors and unclear spacing. Cleaning up the code and commenting each step would improve mathematical communication and reproducibility.
The NNLS method is well chosen to enforce non-negativity, but the student could reflect on how this constraint affects model bias and interpretability.
Variables in the RMSD definition (\(x_i\) and \(\hat{x}_i\)) are not clearly distinguished; explicitly defining observed vs predicted enhances mathematical communication.
The conclusion restates key findings and reflects on limitations, but could be more concise and propose specific next steps or variables for future exploration.
The discussion of new teams and dynamic league structure shows critical reflection on the datasetâs limitations, linking back to reliability of predictions.
The references are relevant and credible, but because they appear after the main text they should not receive content-based annotations.
